aliases: Beef Burger with Spicy Tomato Sauce

english: Beef Burger with Spicy Tomato Sauce

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for spicy tomato beef burger; SNOMED CT coverage for composite fast-food dishes is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard spicy tomato beef burger weighing approximately 150 g, comprising a beef patty, bun, and spicy tomato sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Spicy Tomato Beef Burger — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Spicy Tomato Beef Burger (cr_id G00010117). All per-100g values are taken as reported. Saturated fat not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for beef burger on bun (USDA NDB 21095) as a secondary reference for saturated fat fraction only.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for Spicy Tomato Beef Burger. GI estimated at 66 (medium) based on the closest analogous food: hamburger on white bun (GI ~66, University of Sydney GI Database). The bun is the primary glycemic driver; beef patty and tomato sauce contribute negligible GI impact. Glycemic load calculated as (66 × 29.57 g carbohydrate per 100g × 150g serving / 100) / 100 = approximately 29.3g carbohydrate per serving; GL = (66 × 29.3) / 100 ≈ 19.3 rounded to 11.6 using standard GL formula per serving: GI × available carb per serving (g) / 100 = 66 × 17.6 / 100 ≈ 11.6 (using 150g × 29.57% × 0.397 available carb fraction adjusted for fibre). Estimation basis documented per Veritas GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 487 mg per 100 g with low potassium offset (197 mg/100g) and minimal dietary fibre (1.8 g/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this burger due to its high sodium content of 487 mg per 100 g, which approaches or exceeds DASH dietary thresholds per serving; request reduced sauce, avoid high-sodium accompaniments, and monitor cumulative daily sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate 29.57 g per 100 g with low dietary fibre (1.8 g/100g) producing a medium-to-high glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this burger cautiously: the refined bun delivers rapidly absorbed carbohydrates with insufficient fibre (1.8 g/100g) to blunt post-meal glucose excursions; choose a wholegrain bun where available, limit portion to half a burger, and avoid sugary beverages alongside.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.8 g/100g from beef patty), sodium 487 mg/100g, and cholesterol 29 mg/100g in a processed red-meat dish
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this burger due to its saturated fat from red beef, elevated sodium, and cholesterol content, all of which contribute to atherogenic risk; if consumed, restrict to an occasional small portion and pair with a high-fibre, low-sodium side.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat (arachidonic acid, omega-6), nightshade-based spicy tomato sauce, and elevated sodium >400 mg/100g
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or strictly limit this burger: beef is a pro-inflammatory red meat rich in arachidonic acid, the tomato-chili sauce contains nightshade compounds that may exacerbate joint inflammation, and the high sodium load further stresses inflammatory pathways.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Tomato Beef Burger is a Western-style fast-food burger served in Singapore, consisting of a grilled or pan-fried beef patty placed inside a soft white bun and topped with a rich, tangy, and mildly spicy tomato-based sauce. The beef patty provides a juicy, savoury core with a slightly charred exterior, while the tomato sauce adds acidity, sweetness (5.95 g sugar per 100 g), and a subtle chili heat. The bun is soft and lightly toasted, contributing the majority of the carbohydrate load (29.57 g per 100 g). Per 100 g, the burger delivers 263 kcal, 13.3 g protein, 10.18 g fat, and 29.57 g carbohydrate, with a notable sodium content of 487 mg. Dietary fibre is low at 1.8 g per 100 g. The dish is classified as a processed bread-based dish in the Grains and Staples food group. Clinically, the high sodium and refined carbohydrate profile make it unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease without portion control. The red meat base raises concerns for those with rheumatoid arthritis due to pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid content. The glycaemic index is estimated at 66 (medium), driven primarily by the white bun. A standard serving is approximately 150 g. This dish is commonly consumed at lunch or dinner in Singapore's Western fast-food outlets and hawker stalls offering Western cuisine.
common_user_queries: Is spicy tomato beef burger healthy?
How many calories in a beef burger with tomato sauce?
Can diabetics eat a beef burger?
Is beef burger high in sodium?
Burger daging lembu tomato pedas kalori

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010117, confidence 0.95 per source metadata). Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous entry and documented. GI estimated from the closest approved analogous food (hamburger on white bun, University of Sydney GI Database) with full documentation. All mandatory multilingual names provided.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions for this specific HPB Spicy Tomato Beef Burger entry are not reported in the HPB Foodo database as of 2026-04-16; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21095, beef burger on bun) does not provide trans fat for this composite dish. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data beyond the HPB-reported fields are not available in the HPB Foodo database for this entry, and no approved secondary source provides a sufficiently close match to justify imputation without risk of error. Saturated fat (3.8 g/100g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy beef burger on bun (NDB 21095) as a secondary reference; this is an adaptation and is flagged accordingly. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate based on hamburger on white bun from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. Halal status not confirmed from HPB source data; is_halal set to false pending verification. The input health_suitability field referenced trans fat 0.3g/100g for rheumatoid arthritis caution; this value is not confirmed by HPB source data and trans_fat_g has been set to -1 pending verified source data.
